title: Henckelia rugosa
taxonomy: Henckelia rugosa is classified under the family Gesneriaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the African violet family. This family is further divided into several genera, and Henckelia is one of them. Henckelia rugosa, being a member of this genus, is a type of flowering plant native to Southeast Asia. The plant is often used in horticulture for its attractive foliage and vibrant flowers. As a member of the Gesneriaceae family, Henckelia rugosa shares characteristics with other plants in this family, such as African violets and gloxinias.
uses: Henckelia rugosa is reported to have various medicinal uses, primarily in traditional medicine. The plant is believed to possess antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may be beneficial in treating wounds and infections. In some cultures, it is used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Additionally, the plant is said to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Henckelia rugosa for medicinal purposes.

parts: Henckelia rugosa is known to be a plant species used in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asian cultures. The medicinal parts of Henckelia rugosa are its leaves and roots, which are used to treat various health conditions.
